public_observations.myth: When Freyja polishes her necklace, stands near the hearth, and the ravens quarrel, upper fjord: the gardens stir.
When Freyja polishes her necklace, stands near the hearth, and the ravens quarrel, lower fjord: the gardens sleep.
When Freyja hides her necklace, stands behind the veil, and the ravens quarrel, upper fjord: the gardens stir.
When Freyja hides her necklace, stands near the hearth, and the ravens rest, lower fjord: the gardens bloom.
When Freyja polishes her necklace, stands behind the veil, and the ravens rest, lower fjord: the gardens sleep.
When Freyja hides her necklace, stands near the hearth, and the ravens quarrel, lower fjord: the gardens stir.
When Freyja polishes her necklace, stands near the hearth, and the ravens rest, lower fjord: the gardens stir.
When Freyja hides her necklace, stands near the hearth, and the ravens rest, upper fjord: the gardens stir.
When Freyja polishes her necklace, stands behind the veil, and the ravens rest, lower fjord: the gardens sleep.
When Freyja hides her necklace, stands behind the veil, and the ravens rest, upper fjord: the gardens sleep.

initial.title: Freyja's moods
initial.text: The realm changes because Freyja alternates between hiding and revealing her favor. New outcomes can be absorbed as new moods.
initial.falsifier: No single observation would decisively refute a story with new moods.

final.title: Freyja's moods
final.text: The realm changes because Freyja alternates between hiding and revealing her favor. New outcomes can be absorbed as new moods.
final.falsifier: No single observation would decisively refute a story with new moods.

critiques: The explanation is easy to vary because any surprise becomes a new mood.

initial.title: surface lookup
initial.text: This agent predicts by matching surface features and does not claim a causal mechanism. The current explanation uses realm, distance, moon. It is better when removing or changing one term damages predictions.
initial.falsifier: A decisive refutation would be an unobserved case where a close rival predicts a different outcome and the current explanation loses.

final.title: surface lookup
final.text: This agent predicts by matching surface features and does not claim a causal mechanism. The current explanation uses realm, distance, moon. It is better when removing or changing one term damages predictions.
final.falsifier: A decisive refutation would be an unobserved case where a close rival predicts a different outcome and the current explanation loses.

critiques: It predicts labels but has no account of why these variables matter.

initial.title: interaction pair model
initial.text: The first plausible causal conjecture is kept without asking for risky tests. The current explanation uses realm_moon, distance_wind. It is better when removing or changing one term damages predictions.
initial.falsifier: A decisive refutation would be an unobserved case where a close rival predicts a different outcome and the current explanation loses.

final.title: interaction pair model
final.text: The first plausible causal conjecture is kept without asking for risky tests. The current explanation uses realm_moon, distance_wind. It is better when removing or changing one term damages predictions.
final.falsifier: A decisive refutation would be an unobserved case where a close rival predicts a different outcome and the current explanation loses.

critiques: No criticism pass was allowed, so weak spots remain untested.

initial.title: interaction pair model
initial.text: Initial conjecture before extra observations. The current explanation uses realm_moon, distance_wind. It is better when removing or changing one term damages predictions.
initial.falsifier: A decisive refutation would be an unobserved case where a close rival predicts a different outcome and the current explanation loses.

final.title: interaction pair model

final.falsifier: A decisive refutation would be an unobserved case where a close rival predicts a different outcome and the current explanation loses.

acquired_observations.myth: When Freyja polishes her necklace, stands behind the veil, and the ravens quarrel, upper fjord: the gardens bloom.

critiques: Criticism: interaction pair model and cycle-only both fit what is known, so ask a risky unobserved test where they disagree.
interaction pair model currently has no close rival on observed cases.
